heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 26 January 2025, with precipitation totalling 32.56mm.  The rain fell at 6.1mm per hour at times over the course of the 22.4 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 14.3 hour storm which dumped 20.15mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 36.3 hours on 27 January 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 20.3mm per hour and a total rainfall of 10.72mm.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 14 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 15 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The ongoing rainy stretch, 7 days and counting started on 09 November 2025 and ranks 8th in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest rainy period started on 19 January 2025 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 6 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (180°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 49.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.1°C) and outside temperature (down 1.6°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 305 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 99.1m higher than yesterday, 54.8m lower than the past week, 304.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 561m and a peak of 715m, averaging 710.6m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and very little to no wind will be present. Possible light showers, fog or low cloud is expected, and periods of sunshine are expected. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (92%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
